rationale: Review 1 would likely lead to a better proof-quality cleanup because, in addition to the real `comul_includeRight` `change` issue also caught by Review 2, it identifies the similarly brittle `ext x; induction; simp` proofs for tensor-product map identity/composition and the opaque final `simp` chain in `comul_includeRight`, all grounded in the diff. Its `antipode_comp` criticism is somewhat debatable, but acting on Review 1’s extra concrete proof-structure findings is more likely to improve the PR than Review 2’s narrower and more speculative `simp only` prescription.
